domain: anglerfish esca-Photobacterium symbiosis (host-controlled luminous bacterium in fleshy lure; bacterium generates light, host modulates emission via oxygen blood-flow; bacterium acquired from seawater and genome-reduced when host-locked)
specific_mechanism: Host houses external symbiont in specialized organ; symbiont generates signal autonomously; host controls intensity by attenuation (oxygen gating). Saturating maximum; host cannot exceed; only attenuate. Symbiont is environment-acquired and genome-minimal.
llm_application: Host-gated symbiotic signal-generator module (HGSSGM): frozen pretrained host LLM + small externally-pretrained 'symbiont' module for calibrated emission tasks. Learnable scalar oxygen-gate σ(h) ∈ [0,1] modulates symbiont's emission before addition to host residual. Host attenuation-only control; symbiont parameter count << host; saturating cap. Symbiont acquired from external model zoo, not co-trained.
novelty_claim: Auxiliary classifier heads (DEX, DEACON) are co-trained with host. SignalLLM (2509.17197) is external-tool orchestration. SCRAMBLe (2504.04740) is preference-data generation. None retrieve a frozen-host + externally-acquired-symbiont + attenuation-only-host-control + saturating-cap module at inference time.
